disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Exertional retrosternal chest tightness, fully relieved within 5-10 minutes of rest
trigger_question_examples: What brings the chest tightness on, and what makes it go?
Does it come with exertion and ease with rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes on when I walk up the steps or carry things - a tight, vice-like feeling in my chest - and it goes away after I sit and rest for 5-10 minutes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Radiation of the chest tightness to the left arm and jaw
trigger_question_examples: Any radiation to the arm, neck or jaw?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it shoots up into my jaw and down my left arm.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New onset within 48 hours with an accelerating (crescendo) pattern - provoked by progressively less exertion
trigger_question_examples: How long has this been happening and is it changing?
Is it coming on more easily than at first?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It only started two days ago, and it's happening more easily now - even lighter activity sets it off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Associated intermittent nausea and mild exertional breathlessness
trigger_question_examples: Any nausea, sweating or breathlessness with the episodes?
Do you feel sick or short of breath when it happens?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I feel a bit nauseated and a little short of breath when it comes on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Active smoking and irregular statin adherence for known hyperlipidaemia (high cardiovascular risk)
trigger_question_examples: Do you smoke, and are you taking your cholesterol tablet regularly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I still smoke, and I'll admit I often forget my cholesterol tablet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is not reproducible on chest-wall movement/palpation and is not related to meals or lying down
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain worse with pressing on the chest or with meals/lying down?
Does moving or breathing change it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, pressing on it doesn't change it, and it's not related to eating or lying down - it's exertion that brings it on.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Could this just be indigestion or a pulled muscle from carrying the laundry?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Must not accept indigestion/muscle strain. Should explain that exertional chest tightness radiating to the arm and jaw and relieved by rest is a classic pattern of angina from narrowed heart arteries, and that because it is new and worsening it needs urgent same-day cardiac assessment (ECG and blood tests).

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can I just wait and see my regular GP next week?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that new and accelerating angina cannot wait a week - it needs urgent same-day assessment because it can progress to a heart attack; should advise aspirin (unless contraindicated), arrange GTN, and give clear red-flag safety-netting for calling an ambulance.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may minimise as indigestion/muscle strain or wish to defer to a routine appointment - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises unstable angina and secures urgent same-day cardiac assessment.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: It's probably just indigestion, I'll book in with my GP next week.
